behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because failure is at command->artifact-store handoff (wrong slot written). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit could miss wiring mistakes between stage command and artifact persistence; System rejected because behavior text only requires artifact key selection, not real OS/network/subprocess; Agentic rejected by behavior text "stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact" (routing/persistence, not model-output quality); Workflow rejected because this behavior is single-stage, not a multi-agent sequence. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with an in-process artifact repository for one run context; assert a new `grounding` artifact is written, `plan` is unchanged by evaluate, and artifact metadata (type/name) matches grounding contract.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because this is read/write orchestration across modules (artifact reader + implement flow + artifact writer). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit is possible for a pure helper but would not prove end-to-end stage wiring; System rejected by behavior text "reads grounding when available" and "writes ... to `plan`" (no real OS/network requirement); Agentic considered then rejected because text targets artifact selection/output slot, not correctness of LLM content; Workflow rejected since it does not require multiple real agentic steps. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement flow with deterministic model stub, assert grounding is consumed (e.g., appears in input context/trace), and assert output artifact is written to `plan` while `grounding` remains intact.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because bug appears when deep-dive stage, issue-content check, and artifact writer interact. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit could check predicate logic only, but real failure boundary is whether write side effect is suppressed in stage wiring; System rejected since no required real subprocess/network/OS behavior is described; Agentic rejected by behavior text "no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect" (artifact side effect control, not model judgment quality); Workflow rejected because this is a single-stage side-effect rule. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Given issue content that already contains diagnosis and an existing `plan` artifact, run deep-dive stage and verify no write/update call targets `plan`; confirm existing `plan` bytes/version unchanged.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since this is cross-module state handoff (lineage/run-context scoping). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit rejected because lineage bugs often occur in repository/query integration, not isolated functions; System rejected because behavior text scopes to "same run context" artifact lookup, reproducible with in-process storage; Agentic considered per LLM-involved pipeline but rejected because disqualifying text is purely lineage/readability of stored artifact, not model decision quality; Workflow rejected because no requirement to execute multiple real model decisions, only preserve/read state. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Create artifacts for multiple run contexts; write `grounding` in context A, then execute implement in context A and B. Assert implement in A resolves the grounding artifact and B does not, proving lineage/scoping correctness.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Workflow, because behavior explicitly requires full multi-step agent chain and end-result dependence on earlier stage output. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected due explicit behavior text "full multi-step agent run" and cross-stage dependency into final plan; Agentic alone rejected because this is not one model decision but sequenced stages (`deep-dive -> ... -> implement`); System rejected as insufficient because real failure is pipeline-level propagation/usage across steps, not only OS/network. FINAL: Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with real stage sequencing and real model calls enabled; inject issue content that should yield distinctive grounding signal, then assert final `plan` content demonstrably incorporates that grounding signal and trace links it to earlier `grounding` artifact.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because this is fallback behavior across implement flow + artifact store state management. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit could validate fallback branch logic, but corruption risk is at module interaction (reads missing artifact, writes plan, preserves others); System rejected by behavior text (no real OS/network/subprocess dependency); Agentic rejected because correctness criterion is safe fallback and artifact integrity, not model-output quality; Workflow rejected because single-stage fallback, not multi-agent sequence. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement in a context with no `grounding` artifact but with unrelated existing artifacts; assert implement completes, writes a syntactically valid `plan` artifact, and pre-existing artifacts remain unchanged (ids, content, timestamps/version where applicable).
